description: Fortified bishop's court with cathedral bell towers and kremlin walls at the historic core of Vologda.
facts: Allow time for museum and church opening hours.
The ensemble is a focal point for walking the old centre.

description: Vologda Kremlin (recent view) — landmark in Vologda.
facts: Combine with St Sophia Cathedral and the regional museum nearby.
Crowds peak in summer travel months.

description: Open square beside the kremlin used for events and as the main approach to the ensemble.
facts: Good orientation point before entering museum buildings.
Ice and snow in winter require care on paving.

description: Landmark cathedral domes seen from the Vologda River, defining the city's northern skyline.
facts: River walks link several churches and embankments.
Early light can be excellent for photography.

description: Former Sobornaya Square inside the kremlin, framed by cathedral architecture and open paving.
facts: Central to guided tours of the kremlin.
Respect roped areas and service schedules.

description: Cathedral of the Resurrection — Wikimedia disambiguation page.
facts: Check whether tower climbs are offered seasonally.
Combine with neighbouring kremlin museums.

description: Museum of Lace — landmark in Vologda.
facts: Allow time for temporary exhibitions.
Gift shop may offer certified lace products.

description: Vologda River embankment — landmark in Vologda.
facts: Pleasant for an evening stroll in warmer months.
Links the kremlin area to residential quarters upstream.

description: Major monastery complex north of the centre, with fortified walls and church buildings.
facts: Requires transport or a longer walk from the kremlin.
Confirm opening hours; some areas may close seasonally.

description: Courtyard perspective of domes and walls showing the density of the kremlin churches.
facts: Useful reference for how routes circulate inside the walls.
Museum tickets may bundle several buildings.

description: The Spaso-Prilutsky Monastery (Russian: Спасо-Прилуцкий Димитриев монастырь) is a fortified (walled) Russian Orthodox monastery in the settlement of Priluki on the bank of the Vologda River near the city of Vologda. Its history goes back to the 14th-century missionary activities of St. Sergius of Radonezh and his disciples.

description: The Vologda (Russian: Вологда) is a river in Sheksninsky and Vologodsky Districts of Vologda Oblast as well as in the city of Vologda in Russia. A right-tributary of the Sukhona, it is 155 kilometres (96 mi) long, and the area of its basin 3,030 square kilometres (1,170 sq mi). The principal tributary is the Toshnya (right).
